'''Qid Proko''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] and a survivor of [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]], which [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] issued in an attempt to wipe out the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] following the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Proko managed to evade the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]] and traveled [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] for a [[Time/Legends|time]]. He eventually settled down for a life as a [[Technician/Legends|mechanic]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Poderis]], in the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]].

==Biography==
Qid Proko was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] who served in the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. A follower of the path of a [[Jedi Consular]], Proko served the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] during its [[Republic Classic era|final]] [[Standard year/Legends|years]].<ref name="RES" /> In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], as the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] ended, [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] declared himself [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]], took over the Republic that Proko and the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] had defended, and transformed it into the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> With the issuance of [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]], Palpatine instructed the Republic's [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] to kill their own Jedi commanders; Proko was located in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] at the [[Time/Legends|time]], but he managed to escape.<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|46|Proko, Qid}}</ref> As the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor's]] [[New Order/Legends|New Order]] rose to power, Proko traveled [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] and survived the threat of the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]].<ref name="RES" />

Proko's travels brought him to the storm-swept<ref name="RES" /> [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]]<ref name="TES">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Poderis]], where he made a new life for himself as a [[Technician/Legends|technician]] and worked in a [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bike]] repair shop. Throughout the local settlement, Proko was known as a quiet but honest being. Despite having given up his life as a Jedi for a simpler profession, and having taken to using a [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]], Proko still kept his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] hidden in storage.<ref name="RES" />

==Personality and traits==
In his life after the fall of the Republic, Qid Proko was known as a quiet and honest being. Proko was multilingual: he spoke [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]], [[Quarrenese/Legends|Quarren]], [[Mon Calamari (language)/Legends|Mon Calamarian]], and [[Rodese/Legends|Rodese]].<ref name="RES" /> In the Quarren tongue, Proko's full name meant "quick-witted seeker of lights".<ref name="GCG">''[[Galactic Campaign Guide]]''</ref>

==Powers and abilities==
Among the [[Force power/Legends|Force powers]] Proko mastered during his time as a Jedi were [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinesis]], [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathy]], [[Force healing/Legends|Force healing]], [[Force empathy/Legends|empathy]], and [[Force stealth]].<ref name="RES" />

==Behind the scenes==
Qid Proko was first mentioned in the ''[[Rebellion Era Sourcebook]]'', a supplement for ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' written in [[2001]] by [[Bill Slavicsek]], [[Steve Miller]], and [[Owen K.C. Stephens]]. According to the book, Proko was designed as an example of a Jedi mentor whom player characters might seek out to further their [[Jedi training/Legends|Jedi training]].<ref name="RES" />
